plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall broad index exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk and comply with conservative mode.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income and broad market exposure, and QQQ to 20% for further diversification.
Buy TSLA at 5% as a high-quality long setup to add some growth potential.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall position sizes to manage capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and aims for lower drawdown, though growth potential is limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits and reduce overall broad index exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to remove underperforming leveraged ETFs and reduce overall risk.
Increase CRWD to 15% to add to a strong AI/Software holding with good momentum.
Buy TSLA at 8% as a high-scoring new long setup to add quality growth.
Increase QQQI to 22% to maintain income and broad market exposure while rebalancing.
The overall goal is to blend broad index with quality tech and manage leverage effectively.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces concentration and leverage while blending broad exposure with quality tech, aiming for moderate drawdown.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits, freeing capital for higher-conviction plays.
Add to SOXL to 5% to increase leveraged semiconductor exposure, leveraging its high scanner score.
Sell all SPAL to exit an underperforming leveraged ETF and reallocate capital.
Increase CRWD to 20% to concentrate in a strong AI/Software holding with excellent momentum.
Buy TSLA at 10% as a high-scoring new long setup to add significant growth potential.
Increase SMH to 15% to further lean into the semiconductor sector's strength.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It embraces higher leverage and concentration, which significantly raises both upside potential and drawdown risk, especially with leveraged ETFs' daily decay.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall broad index exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate all leveraged ETFs and reduce high-beta exposure.
Increase QQQI to 35% to maximize income-focused ETF allocation for steady distributions.
Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce growth-oriented exposure and reallocate towards income.
Trim CRWD to 5% to reduce exposure to a high-beta single name.
The plan prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth, maintaining a cash buffer.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, focused on parking cash for yield. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income through income and broad index ETFs, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
